skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: In State Y, Ms. Lin's tenth-grade class of 25 students was surveyed, and 40 percent reported owning a pet dog. The average tenth-grade class size in State Y is 25. If the students in Ms. Lin's class are representative of students in the state's tenth-grade classes and there are 1,500 tenth-grade classes in the state, which of the following best estimates the number of tenth-grade students in the state who do NOT own a pet dog?
rationale: Total students: 25 × 1,500 = 37,500. The proportion who do NOT own a pet dog is 100% − 40% = 60%. So 0.6 × 37,500 = 22,500. Choice A is 0.4 × 37,500 (those who own). Choice C is the total. Choice D is 0.6 × 1,500 (number of classes × nonowners proportion).
